title: Adding your first product
subtitle: Name, type, category, and distribution model

products.title: Navigate to the Products page
products.body: From the sidebar, click Products. This shows all products registered in your organisation. To add a new one, click the Add Product button.

name.title: Name and describe your product
name.sub: Use the name your customers know it by
name.body:
Enter your product's name and a brief description explaining what the product does. This information appears in your Technical File and Declaration of Conformity.

Use the commercial product name, not an internal code name. This is what appears on compliance documents.

type.title: Select the product type
type.sub: What kind of software product is it?
type.body:
Choose the type that best describes your product:

  • Web Application
  • Desktop Application
  • Mobile Application
  • Library / Component
  • Firmware
  • Operating System
  • IoT Device

category.title: Choose the CRA risk category
category.sub: This determines your conformity assessment route
category.body:
The CRA classifies products into four risk categories per Art. 6-8 and Annex III. This determines which conformity assessment procedure applies (Art. 32):

  • Default self-assessment. Most software products fall here.
  • Important Class I self-assessment if harmonised standards are applied; otherwise third-party assessment
  • Important Class II mandatory third-party assessment by a notified body
  • Critical mandatory EU-level certification
If unsure, start with Default and use the AI Category Recommender later to verify. You can change the category at any time. Changing it recalculates your conformity assessment route and applicable obligations.

distmodel.title: Set the distribution model
distmodel.body:
Select how your product is distributed to users:

  • SaaS / Hosted
  • Binary distribution
  • Source available
  • Library / Component
  • Internal only

The distribution model affects which licence compliance rules apply to your dependencies.

created.title: Product created
created.sub: Next step: connect your source code repository
created.body:
Your product is now registered in CRANIS2. The next step is to connect your source code repository. This enables CRANIS2 to automatically generate your SBOM (Art. 13(5)), scan for vulnerabilities (Art. 13(6)), and build your compliance evidence.

Navigate to the product's Overview tab and click Connect Repository. The help panel will show the connection guide.

recommender.title: AI Category Recommender
recommender.sub: Not sure which category? Let CRANIS2 help
recommender.body:
If you are unsure which CRA category applies to your product, CRANIS2 includes a Category Recommender (prompt key: category_recommendation, Pro plan) that asks four simple questions and suggests the correct classification based on Art. 6-8 and Annex III.

You can run the recommender at any time by clicking the CRA category badge on your product's detail page. You review and approve the recommendation before it is applied.
